summary: Keep AWS Config recording useful: tune snapshot delivery frequency, deploy and tune AWS-managed guardrail rules, retag rules, and retire a rule. Enabling or disabling the recorder and rewriting the recording strategy stay engineer-only — they govern account-wide compliance capture.

operations.title: Change Config snapshot delivery frequency
operations.summary: Change how often AWS Config writes a full configuration snapshot to S3.
operations.description: Update how often AWS Config writes a full configuration snapshot to the awsconfig-baseline-config bucket by setting snapshot_delivery_properties.delivery_frequency on the delivery channel. Purely a cadence change — no impact on continuous change recording or on config-rule evaluation.

params.name: channel
params.label: Config delivery channel
params.help: The account's AWS Config delivery channel to retune.

params.name: new_delivery_frequency
params.label: Snapshot delivery frequency
params.help: How often a full config snapshot is delivered to S3. More frequent snapshots increase S3 object volume and cost.

operations.title: Deploy an AWS-managed Config guardrail rule
operations.summary: Add an AWS-managed compliance rule. It only reports non-compliant resources — it doesn't fix or block anything.
operations.description: Add a new aws_config_config_rule backed by an AWS-managed source identifier (e.g. S3_BUCKET_PUBLIC_READ_PROHIBITED) so the recorder starts evaluating that guardrail across the estate. Adds a compliance control only — it reports NON_COMPLIANT resources but does not remediate or block anything on its own.

params.name: recorder
params.label: Configuration recorder
params.help: The active recorder the new rule evaluates against; Config must be recording for the rule to produce results.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: Rule name
params.help: Terraform-safe, unique rule name. Lowercase-with-hyphens recommended, max 128 chars.

params.name: source_identifier
params.label: Managed rule
params.help: AWS-managed rule to deploy. Restricted to a vetted guardrail set for this estate.

params.name: input_parameters
params.label: Rule parameters (JSON)
params.help: Optional managed-rule parameters as a JSON object, e.g. {"blockedPort1":"22"}. Leave empty for rules that take none.

operations.title: Retune a Config rule's parameters
operations.summary: Retune a compliance rule's parameters, like a tighter threshold. Only changes what counts as compliant, not the resources.
operations.description: Update the input_parameters on an existing managed Config rule (for example tightening maxAccessKeyAge or a required tag key). Changes only which resources evaluate as compliant; the resources themselves are untouched.

params.name: rule
params.label: Config rule
params.help: The managed Config rule whose parameters you want to change.

params.name: new_input_parameters
params.label: New rule parameters (JSON)
params.help: Full replacement JSON object of parameter keys/values for this rule, e.g. {"maxAccessKeyAge":"90"}. Replaces the existing parameter set.

operations.title: Update tags on a Config rule
operations.summary: Update this rule's tags. Metadata only — no effect on how it evaluates compliance.
operations.description: Set or replace the tags map on an existing Config rule for ownership, cost and governance attribution. Metadata-only change with no effect on rule evaluation or compliance results.

params.name: rule
params.label: Config rule
params.help: The Config rule to retag.

params.name: new_tags
params.label: Tags
params.help: Full replacement set of key/value tags, e.g. {"Owner":"SecOps","CostCenter":"Platform"}.

operations.title: Retire a Config guardrail rule
operations.summary: Permanently retire this compliance rule and its history. This can't be undone, so a second approver signs off first.
operations.description: Destroy an existing aws_config_config_rule, permanently stopping its evaluations and dropping its compliance history for the estate. Removing a security/compliance guardrail is not reversible by simply re-adding — historical evaluation data is lost — so it needs two approvals.

params.name: rule
params.label: Config rule to remove
params.help: The managed Config rule to permanently delete. Its compliance history is not recoverable.

operations.title: Toggle recording of all supported resource types
operations.summary: Turn recording on or off for every supported resource type. Narrowing coverage can leave compliance gaps.
operations.description: Enable or disable recording_group.all_supported so the recorder captures every supported resource type. In-place, reversible config change with no downtime; narrowing coverage may create a compliance gap, hence guardrails.

params.name: recorder
params.label: Configuration recorder
params.help: The account's AWS Config configuration recorder to update, picked from the estate inventory so only the existing Terraform-managed recorder can be selected.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Record all supported types
params.help: true makes the recorder capture configuration changes for every resource type AWS Config supports; false narrows recording coverage, which can create compliance gaps — a bounded true/false toggle that is reversible.

operations.title: Toggle recording of global resource types
operations.summary: Turn on or off recording of account-wide resources like IAM, in addition to regional ones.
operations.description: Enable or disable recording_group.include_global_resource_types so the recorder captures global resources (IAM, WAF, etc.). In-place, reversible config change with no downtime.

params.name: recorder
params.label: Configuration recorder
params.help: The account's AWS Config configuration recorder to update, picked from the estate inventory of Terraform-managed recorders.

params.name: enabled
params.label: Record global resource types
params.help: true also records global (non-regional) resource types such as IAM into this recorder's configuration history; false skips them — a bounded true/false toggle that is fully reversible.

operations.title: Set recorder recording frequency
operations.summary: Set whether AWS Config records changes continuously or once a day. Daily cuts cost but is less detailed.
operations.description: Set recording_mode.recording_frequency to CONTINUOUS or DAILY, controlling how often Config records configuration changes. In-place, reversible, no downtime; DAILY reduces cost and recording granularity.

params.name: recorder
params.label: Configuration recorder
params.help: The account's AWS Config configuration recorder whose recording cadence to change, picked from the estate inventory.

params.name: frequency
params.label: Recording frequency
params.help: How often AWS Config records configuration changes: CONTINUOUS captures each change as it happens, while DAILY batches them once a day for lower cost at coarser granularity — restricted to these two AWS-supported modes.

operations.title: Change the IAM service role used by the recorder
operations.summary: Point the recorder at a different IAM role. If that role can't be assumed by Config, recording stalls.
operations.description: Point role_arn at a different existing IAM role (picked from inventory) that Config assumes to record configuration. In-place and reversible with no downtime; the chosen role must trust config.amazonaws.com or recording will stall, hence guardrails.

params.name: recorder
params.label: Configuration recorder
params.help: The account's AWS Config configuration recorder whose service role to change, picked from the estate inventory.

params.name: role
params.label: IAM service role
params.help: The existing IAM role AWS Config assumes to read and record resource configurations, picked from inventory so no new role or policy can be minted here; the role must trust config.amazonaws.com or recording will stall.

operations.title: Request another change to AWS Config configuration recorder
operations.summary: Request any other change to the configuration recorder; an engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.
operations.description: Escalation path for any change to the configuration recorder not offered as a bounded self-service form — e.g. editing recording_group.resource_types, exclusion_by_resource_types, recording_strategy.use_only, recording_mode_override blocks, name (replacement), or the region override. An engineer authors and applies the Terraform.

params.name: recorder
params.label: Configuration recorder
params.help: The configuration recorder to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Edit rule description
operations.summary: Edit this rule's description. Just documentation — it doesn't change what the rule evaluates.
operations.description: Update the free-text description of a Config rule. Cosmetic metadata only, no effect on evaluation behaviour. In-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: rule
params.label: Config rule
params.help: The Config rule whose description to edit, picked from the estate inventory; the description is documentation only and does not change what the rule evaluates.

params.name: description
params.label: Description
params.help: Up to 256 characters. Purely descriptive; does not change what the rule evaluates.

operations.title: Set rule maximum execution frequency
operations.summary: Set how often this rule re-checks compliance, from hourly up to once a day.
operations.description: Set maximum_execution_frequency for a periodic Config rule to a bounded AWS enum. Controls how often the rule re-evaluates; reversible, no downtime. (Applies to periodic-trigger rules.)

params.name: rule
params.label: Config rule
params.help: The periodic-trigger Config rule to retune, picked from the estate inventory so only existing Terraform-managed rules can be selected.

params.name: frequency
params.label: Maximum execution frequency
params.help: How often AWS Config re-evaluates the rule.

operations.title: Add an evaluation mode
operations.summary: Set whether this rule checks resources only after they deploy, or beforehand too. Not every rule supports both.
operations.description: Append an evaluation_mode block (DETECTIVE or PROACTIVE) to a Config rule. In-place, reversible; some rules only support DETECTIVE, so apply may error harmlessly if the mode is unsupported. Guarded.

params.name: rule
params.label: Config rule
params.help: The Config rule to add the evaluation mode to, picked from the estate inventory of Terraform-managed rules.

params.name: mode
params.label: Evaluation mode
params.help: DETECTIVE evaluates deployed resources; PROACTIVE evaluates before deployment (only supported by some managed rules).

operations.title: Add a tag to a Config rule
operations.summary: Add a tag to this rule. Metadata only — no effect on how it runs.
operations.description: Add a single key/value entry to the rule's tags map. Cosmetic metadata, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: rule
params.label: Config rule
params.help: The Config rule to tag, picked from the estate inventory; tags are metadata only and never change rule evaluation or compliance results.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: 1-128 chars: letters, numbers, and _ . : / + - @ (no spaces or =)

params.name: value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: 0-256 chars: letters, numbers, spaces and _ . : / = + - @

operations.title: Remove a tag from a Config rule
operations.summary: Remove a tag from this rule. Metadata only — no effect on how it runs.
operations.description: Remove a single key from the rule's tags map by key. Cosmetic metadata, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: rule
params.label: Config rule
params.help: The Config rule whose tag to remove, picked from the estate inventory; removing a tag is metadata-only and reversible.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key to remove
params.help: Exact key of the tag entry to delete.

operations.title: Request another change to Config rule
operations.summary: Request any other change to this compliance rule; an engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.
operations.description: Catch-all for any change not offered as a bounded form (e.g. source/source_identifier, scope resource-type filters, input_parameters JSON, or a rule rename). An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.

params.name: rule
params.label: Config rule
params.help: The Config rule this request is about, when the estate already tracks one. Leave blank when describing one that is not tracked yet.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Set config snapshot delivery frequency
operations.summary: Change how often this delivery channel exports a full configuration snapshot to S3.
operations.description: Update snapshot_delivery_properties.delivery_frequency on an AWS Config delivery channel. Bounded enum, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: channel
params.label: Delivery channel
params.help: The AWS Config delivery channel to retune, picked from the estate inventory (the account runs a single channel writing snapshots to the baseline S3 bucket).

params.name: delivery_frequency
params.label: Snapshot delivery frequency
params.help: How often AWS Config delivers configuration snapshots. Higher frequency means more deliveries.

operations.title: Set delivery channel S3 key prefix
operations.summary: Change the folder prefix used for this channel's exported snapshots in S3.
operations.description: Update s3_key_prefix — the object key prefix under the destination bucket. Pattern-bounded string, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: channel
params.label: Delivery channel
params.help: The AWS Config delivery channel whose S3 key prefix to change, picked from the estate inventory so only the existing Terraform-managed channel can be selected.

params.name: s3_key_prefix
params.label: S3 key prefix
params.help: Object key prefix under the destination bucket. Alphanumerics and !_.*'()/- , up to 1024 chars.

operations.title: Set delivery channel destination S3 bucket
operations.summary: Point this delivery channel at a different S3 bucket. That bucket must allow AWS Config to write to it.
operations.description: Update s3_bucket_name to a different existing bucket for configuration history/snapshots. Bounded by inventory picker, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: channel
params.label: Delivery channel
params.help: The AWS Config delivery channel whose destination bucket to change, picked from the estate inventory of Terraform-managed channels.

params.name: s3_bucket_name
params.label: Destination S3 bucket
params.help: Existing S3 bucket that receives AWS Config snapshots. Bucket policy must permit AWS Config delivery.

operations.title: Set delivery channel SNS notification topic
operations.summary: Change which SNS topic gets notified by this delivery channel.
operations.description: Update sns_topic_arn — the SNS topic AWS Config publishes notifications to. Bounded by inventory picker, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: channel
params.label: Delivery channel
params.help: The AWS Config delivery channel whose notification topic to change, picked from the estate inventory of Terraform-managed channels.

params.name: sns_topic_arn
params.label: SNS topic
params.help: Existing SNS topic that receives AWS Config notifications.

operations.title: Request another change to AWS Config delivery channel
operations.summary: Request any other change to this delivery channel; an engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.
operations.description: Catch-all for any AWS Config delivery channel change not offered as a bounded form (e.g. s3_kms_key_arn, name/region replacement). An engineer authors the Terraform.

params.name: channel
params.label: Delivery channel
params.help: The delivery channel to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Provision a configuration recorder
operations.summary: Create the AWS Config recorder using a chosen service role. Narrowing or disabling it blinds compliance.
operations.description: Create an aws_config_configuration_recorder recording all supported resource types with an IAM role chosen from inventory. Config recording underpins compliance detection, so the recording group is set to all-supported and the role's read coverage is confirmed at review. Submitting drafts the resource and routes it to an engineer to review and finish.

params.name: recorder_name
params.label: Recorder name
params.help: The name of the recorder (written to the name attribute); AWS Config conventionally uses one recorder named default.

params.name: role_arn
params.label: Config role
params.help: The IAM role Config assumes to read resource configuration (the aws_iam_role.arn it references).

params.name: all_supported
params.label: All supported
params.help: Record every supported resource type; written into the draft's recording_group.

params.name: include_global_resource_types
params.label: Include global resource types
params.help: Include global resource types (such as IAM) in recording; written into the draft's recording_group.

decisions: Config records with the selected role: confirm the role grants read access to every resource type in scope, since a narrower role silently skips resources from compliance evaluation.
Disabling or narrowing the recorder blinds compliance detection across the account; scope changes are engineer-reviewed, never self-service.

operations.title: Deploy a managed Config rule
operations.summary: Deploy an AWS-managed Config guardrail rule. Custom Guard policy rules are out of self-service reach.
operations.description: Create an aws_config_config_rule that evaluates resources against an AWS-managed rule (source owner AWS). A custom-policy rule that runs engineer-authored Guard policy text is never offered here. The managed rule identifier is chosen from a short allowlist of common guardrails. Submitting drafts the resource and routes it to an engineer to review and finish.

params.name: rule_name
params.label: Rule name
params.help: The name of the rule (written to the name attribute).

params.name: source_owner
params.label: Rule owner
params.help: Always AWS for a managed guardrail rule; custom-policy rules are out of self-service reach.

params.name: source_identifier
params.label: Managed rule
params.help: The AWS-managed rule to deploy. A short allowlist of common guardrails is offered; leave it as engineer-decides to have the engineer pick another managed rule.

params.name: description
params.label: Description
params.help: A short note describing what the rule checks for.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: Description
params.help: A short description recorded as the resource's Description tag.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: The team or person accountable for this resource, recorded as its PIC tag.

decisions: This form deploys AWS-managed guardrail rules only (source owner AWS). A custom-policy rule that runs engineer-authored Guard policy text is out of self-service reach.
Confirm the rule's scope and parameters match the intent; a mis-scoped rule reports false compliance and the engineer sets any input parameters at review.
